metadata.description: Paper-Preparation Package: Reference Fixes, Text Patches, and Collapsed Partial Correlation for Arrhenius Kinetics of LLM Inference

reference_corrections.duplicate_description: Duplicate entry for TIGER-Lab/MMLU-Pro dataset paper; identical to reference [13]. Entry [14] removed; references [15..19] renumbered to [14..18].
reference_corrections.smart_citation_note: SMART (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound) citation at [12] is unaffected by the renumbering because it precedes the removed entry [14]. No change needed.
reference_corrections.affected_in_text_citations: All in-text citations of [14] should be replaced with [13]. In-text citations of [15], [16], [17], [18], [19] should be decremented by 1 to [14], [15], [16], [17], [18].

text_patches.P1_mechanistic_repositioning: We reframe our contribution as an empirical characterisation rather than a mechanistic claim. The Arrhenius analogy is used as a parametric scaffold that yields a compact, interpretable summary (E_a, ...
text_patches.P2_theorem6_NgtK_caveat: Theorem 6 (T_thresh lower-bound guarantee) now carries an explicit caveat: 'The lower-bound guarantee holds when N > K (sample size exceeds answer choices). For N ≤ K the empirical minimum temperature...
text_patches.P3_pilot_gate_rationale: The pilot gate threshold (fraction of rising-limb instances ≥ 0.30) was pre-registered as a feasibility check to avoid fitting the Arrhenius model on populations where P(correct|T) is uniformly zero. ...
text_patches.P4_partial_correlation_df_caveat: We replace the 14-category partial Spearman (df ≈ 15) with a collapsed 3-category version (STEM / Social+Humanities / Other) that yields df = 27. Result: partial ρ = 0.592 (95% CI [0.288, 0.788], p = ...
text_patches.P5_CoT_future_direction: The catalysis (CoT) analysis is demoted from a main result to a future-direction paragraph. The current evidence (1/3 valid instances showing CoT-induced E_a reduction) is insufficient to support a cl...
text_patches.P6_subset_rho_T_pref_lt1: Among the 17 instances where T_pref < 1.0 (i.e., where temperature routing adds practical value over Fixed T = 1.0), the Spearman correlation ρ(E_a, T_pref) = 0.752. This quantifies where the Arrheniu...
